rationale:
I verified the implementation against every frozen Task 4a requirement (v13 = v12 + AM-40) and find it faithful, within scope, with no reinterpretation.

(a) CLOSED INVENTORY: PACKAGE_ROOTS is exactly the five spine packages and PACKAGE_ROOTS_EXCLUDE exactly the three sibling products per AM-40; test-inventory §7 mechanically discovers tracked package.json directories via `git ls-files`, proves discovered == union with bidirectional set checks + size equality, proves disjointness, and pins the exact sorted arrays — a new package fails until classified, and a tracked repo-root package.json is asserted absent rather than silently dropped. DOC_ROOTS, docs/runs and docs/runs/clotho-self-weave exclusions, CONTRACT_FILES (D31, sorted, existence-checked), RUN_SOURCES, five weaver ids with integer versions: all committed. REQUIRED_INVENTORY_IDS matches the frozen normative table exactly (git->[package-files,package-symbols], code->[package-modules], test->[package-manifests,test-files], doc->[doc-files], ledger->[contract-files,ledger-sources,run-sources]) with a deepEqual unit against an independently restated NORMATIVE literal, plus sorted/unique/deep-freeze assertions. LOADER_CAPABLE_BUILTIN_SAFE_EXPORTS is deep-frozen with exactly {module, node:module} -> ["builtinModules","isBuiltin"], with deep-equal, exact-key/value, deep-freeze, and mutation-fails-and-leaves-unchanged units. LEDGER_SOURCES=[] is a committed exact-final reviewed value (empty set), documented and pinned by a unit — a closed source inventory, not a deferred placeholder; I judge this within the D17/AM-17 reading the artifact states.

(b) D17/AM-17: WEAVER_IMPL_FILES commits only git ({registry, git}) and code ({registry, code, util}) closures; test-inventory asserts every named file exists; test-closure §1 proves equality against derived closures and §12 asserts the exact derived lists directly including registry as a closure leaf, so the fixed points are genuine.

(c) UTIL SUBSTRATE: ONE classifier (classifyModuleLoads) + ONE resolver (resolveRelativeSpecifier) exported and consumed by both the closure derivation and the code weaver; test-closure §9 proves single-definition-in-util by scanning all clotho modules, proves deriveAcceptedClosure's own body calls both by name, proves code.mjs imports the shared resolver, and forbids any second extension-appending resolver — AM-34 test 19 satisfied for the consumers that exist at this PR (the advisory scanner is Task 5, correctly deferred). The lexer is dependency-free, comment/string/template/regex-aware with sound regex-vs-division handling (control-head parens, postfix ++/--, `of` division) and escape decoding so an escaped-relative specifier cannot dodge the closure. Phase-1 export grammar is exactly the four forms; every unsupported category (default, star, list/re-export, destructuring, let/var) warns and emits no symbol, with property-name lookalikes correctly silent. Walker rejects root escape, symlinked roots/components/ancestors, and symlinked entries (fail-closed throw, not skip). Counted iterator counts only on completed consumption (partial not counted, exhaustion only on natural completion, cardinality = list length) and units prove accounting is unreachable from the iterable including symbol-keyed probes. physicalContainment lstats every component including repo-root ancestors, rejects symlink components, resolves only the deepest existing ancestor, never follows a symlink to decide; units cover symlinked allowed root, symlinked nested parent, escape-via-symlink-target, and plain-path acceptance. Git wrapper permits exactly the five frozen shapes via execFileSync with no shell, order-sensitive flag matching, path args constrained to canonical repo-relative non-option-shaped POSIX, and gitSpawnOptions makes cwd/shell/encoding non-overridable; validation-before-spawn is proven.

(d) TEST-CLOSURE: derives closures with the shared classifier/resolver and proves committed inventories equal (never trusted) through the ONE harness also used by the failure fixtures. Per-form omission fixtures cover named import, side-effect, export{}from, export * from, export * as ns from, dynamic import(), require(), module.require(), keyword-named alias, and whitespace module.require; recursion/cycle/unreachable-dynamic-import; permitted merkle-dag traversal; missing/extra/nonexistent-inventory failures; unresolved, ambiguous-extension, non-regular, escape, forbidden, symlink (junctions that must run, not skip), and entry-level checks; non-literal and comment/string lookalikes create no edge.

(e) GIT WEAVER: exact frozen argv shapes (proven by recorded mock calls), first-result (earliest) selection, source_ref git:<same-sha>, every line validated as full SHA with blank/internal-blank/non-SHA/bare-CR fatal and only genuinely-empty output warning with no edge; CRLF tolerance is a reasonable platform-terminator reading, and the distinction is pinned by units. -S token verbatim with metacharacters. Both counted sources exhausted including the empty-source case.

(f) CODE WEAVER: all four depends-on endpoint shapes proven with locator-field assertions (repository_ref, blob_sha, symbol); alias resolution; no-export named import preserved as repository-file -> code-symbol; used-but-non-seeded name threads at file level, not unrepresentable; unrepresentable-consumer ONLY for unresolvable specifiers (missing .mjs or resolving outside closed roots), with non-.mjs specifiers silent (a defensible reading of the explicit-.mjs grammar: outside the grammar, not unresolvable — and the artifact pins this precedence deliberately); symlink/escape targets fail closed rather than downgrade; unused imports and comment/string/regex non-uses emit no edge; dedup; byte-equal {edges,warnings} over two runs for both weavers.

CONSTRAINTS: zero runtime deps (gate), ESM, imports limited to node:/clotho-relative/vendor (gate), diff confined to clotho/ (gate: spine read-only), repository_ref derived never hardcoded, weavers emit no time/counts/signatures (D5), producer==attribution fields correct (D10). Orchestrator inventory, atomic publication, D34 re-derivation, and test/doc/ledger weavers are correctly absent as later tasks. Deterministic gate reports meets with all 15 checks green.

Minor observations, none rising to a faithfulness defect: (1) DOC_WEAVER_EXCLUDE/GLOBAL_EXCLUDE are committed but unconsumed until 4b/5 — explicitly documented as deferred wiring, and committing the exclusions now is what the task text requires; (2) parseShaLines CRLF acceptance slightly liberalizes "every output line is validated as a full SHA" but is unit-pinned and bare-CR-fatal, preserving the malformed-output-fatal guarantee; (3) test-closure §9's textual regression guards (regex over source) are advisory reinforcement on top of behavioral proofs, not a substitute for them. I stake the seat's signature on approval as-is.
